prevalence.source: WHO HSV seroprevalence context; Indian STI surveillance summaries
prevalence.trend: stable; HSV-2 higher in high-risk groups

burden.dalyPerCapita: low mortality; recurrent morbidity and stigma

epidemiology.diagnosticCriteria: Clinical lesions with PCR/NAAT confirmation where available; type-specific serology for uncertain cases

publicHealthSystem.estimatedPatients: Tens of millions (HSV-1 and HSV-2 combined)
publicHealthSystem.controlRate: Moderate; depends on access to antivirals and counseling

regionalSpecificities.diagnosisCriteria: Clinical diagnosis common; PCR testing limited to tertiary centers
regionalSpecificities.treatmentProtocol: Acyclovir episodic or suppressive therapy; counseling on transmission prevention; pregnancy management to reduce neonatal risk
regionalSpecificities.regionalChallenges: Stigma and low awareness; limited laboratory confirmation outside metros
